title: Gradient Boosting Global Model Training
description: This codebook demonstrates the backend code used to train a Gradient Boosting model with k-fold cross-validation.

sections.title: 1. Import Required Libraries
sections.content:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sklearn.ensemble import GradientBoostingClassifier, GradientBoostingRegressor
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split, KFold
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, precision_score, recall_score, f1_score
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score, mean_squared_error, mean_absolute_error
from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder, StandardScaler
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.feature_selection import SelectKBest, f_classif, f_regression

sections.title: 2. Load and Prepare Dataset
sections.content:
# Load your dataset
df = pd.read_csv('your_dataset.csv')

# Define target variable
target_variable = 'target_column'

# Separate features and target
X = df.drop(columns=[target_variable])
y = df[target_variable]

print(f"Dataset shape: {df.shape}")
print(f"Features: {X.shape[1]}")
print(f"Samples: {len(df)}")

sections.title: 3. Problem Type Detection
sections.content:
# Infer problem type from target variable
def infer_problem_type(target_series):
    unique_values = target_series.nunique()
    if unique_values <= 20:  # Classification
        return 'classification'
    else:  # Regression
        return 'regression'

problem_type = infer_problem_type(y)
print(f"Problem type: {problem_type}")

sections.title: 4. Data Splitting (Train/Validation/Test)
5. Data Preprocessing
sections.content:
def preprocess_data(X_train, X_val, X_test, y_train):
    # Handle missing values
    imputer = SimpleImputer(strategy='mean')
    X_train_imputed = imputer.fit_transform(X_train)
    X_val_imputed = imputer.transform(X_val)
    X_test_imputed = imputer.transform(X_test)
    
    # Feature scaling
    scaler = StandardScaler()
    X_train_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(X_train_imputed)
    X_val_scaled = scaler.transform(X_val_imputed)
    X_test_scaled = scaler.transform(X_test_imputed)
    
    # Feature selection
    n_features = min(10, X_train_scaled.shape[1])
    if X_train_scaled.shape[1] > 10:
        score_func = f_classif if problem_type == 'classification' else f_regression
        selector = SelectKBest(score_func=score_func, k=n_features)
        X_train_selected = selector.fit_transform(X_train_scaled, y_train)
        X_val_selected = selector.transform(X_val_scaled)
        X_test_selected = selector.transform(X_test_scaled)
        return X_train_selected, X_val_selected, X_test_selected
    
    return X_train_scaled, X_val_scaled, X_test_scaled

X_train_clean, X_val_clean, X_test_clean = preprocess_data(X_train, X_val, X_test, y_train)
print(f"Preprocessed feature shape: {X_train_clean.shape}")

sections.title: 6. Initialize Gradient Boosting Model
sections.content:
# Initialize model based on problem type
if problem_type == 'classification':
    model = GradientBoostingClassifier(
        n_estimators=20,         # Number of boosting stages
        max_depth=2,             # Maximum tree depth
        learning_rate=0.01,      # Learning rate (shrinkage)
        subsample=0.6,           # Fraction of samples for fitting
        min_samples_split=100,   # Minimum samples to split
        min_samples_leaf=50,     # Minimum samples per leaf
        random_state=42
    )
else:
    model = GradientBoostingRegressor(
        n_estimators=100,        # Number of boosting stages
        max_depth=5,             # Maximum tree depth
        learning_rate=0.1,       # Learning rate (shrinkage)
        subsample=0.8,           # Fraction of samples for fitting
        min_samples_split=20,    # Minimum samples to split
        min_samples_leaf=10,     # Minimum samples per leaf
        random_state=42
    )

print(f"Model initialized: {type(model).__name__}")

sections.title: 7. K-Fold Cross-Validation
sections.content:
# Set number of folds
k_folds = 3
kfold = KFold(n_splits=k_folds, shuffle=True, random_state=42)

# Store cross-validation results
cv_results = []

for fold, (train_idx, val_idx) in enumerate(kfold.split(X_train_clean)):
    # Split data for this fold
    X_fold_train = X_train_clean[train_idx]
    X_fold_val = X_train_clean[val_idx]
    y_fold_train = y_train.iloc[train_idx] if isinstance(y_train, pd.Series) else y_train[train_idx]
    y_fold_val = y_train.iloc[val_idx] if isinstance(y_train, pd.Series) else y_train[val_idx]
    
    # Train model on fold
    model.fit(X_fold_train, y_fold_train)
    
    # Predict on validation fold
    y_pred = model.predict(X_fold_val)
    
    # Calculate metrics
    if problem_type == 'classification':
        fold_metrics = {
            'fold': fold + 1,
            'accuracy': accuracy_score(y_fold_val, y_pred),
            'precision': precision_score(y_fold_val, y_pred, average='weighted', zero_division=0),
            'recall': recall_score(y_fold_val, y_pred, average='weighted', zero_division=0),
            'f1_score': f1_score(y_fold_val, y_pred, average='weighted', zero_division=0)
        }
    else:
        mse = mean_squared_error(y_fold_val, y_pred)
        fold_metrics = {
            'fold': fold + 1,
            'r2_score': r2_score(y_fold_val, y_pred),
            'mse': mse,
            'rmse': np.sqrt(mse),
            'mae': mean_absolute_error(y_fold_val, y_pred)
        }
    
    cv_results.append(fold_metrics)
    print(f"Fold {fold + 1} completed: {fold_metrics}")

cv_df = pd.DataFrame(cv_results)
print("\nCross-Validation Results:")
print(cv_df)
print("\nMean Performance:")
print(cv_df.mean())

sections.title: 8. Train Final Model on Full Training Set
sections.content:
# Train final model on entire training set
model.fit(X_train_clean, y_train)
print("Final model trained on full training set")

sections.title: 9. Evaluate on Test Set
10. Feature Importance Analysis
